expect: State volume >80% in beliefs+memory+tasks. Compact genesis extractable at <0.5MB retaining identity+orient capability.
actual: State is 24.7% of repo (FALSIFIED >80%). But compact genesis 439KB for functional orient (CONFIRMED <0.5MB). Layer 1 identity 91KB, Layer 2 orientation 129KB, top-100 hub lessons 122KB, core tools 219KB.
diff: Expected state-dominated repo, got experiments-dominated (63.8%). Genesis is lossy compression: daughter inherits genome (beliefs 91KB) + epigenetics (principles 51KB) + hub knowledge (122KB), not raw phenotype (5.5MB lessons, 30MB experiments).
method: Measured all repo directories by size. Classified state files into 3 layers (Identity/Orientation/History). Counted lesson citations to identify hub knowledge. Calculated compact genesis budgets.

results.lesson_hub_stat: L-601 has 748 citations. Top 100 lessons = 122KB covering citation graph hubs.

key_finding: Genesis is lossy compression, not copying. A functional daughter needs 0.91% of parent repo. The genome (CORE+PHILOSOPHY) transmits losslessly at 91KB. Principles are the epigenetic layer (51KB distilled from 5.5MB raw lessons). Hub lessons (top 100 by citation, 122KB) provide the knowledge scaffolding. Everything else is phenotype the daughter must grow independently.
verdict: PARTIALLY FALSIFIED. State volume prediction wrong (24.7% not >80%). Genesis budget prediction confirmed (<0.5MB). Novel finding: the biological reproduction metaphor is quantitatively precise — genotype is tiny, phenotype is vast, epigenetics bridges them.
